creator: handy@physics.montana.edu
text:
asapm is Afterstep's answer to XBATT for laptop users.  Looks nicer, more
bells and whistles, gives you a vague guess as to remaining battery life.
(On my TP560, it claims total life is about 1:40...which, if I don't do 
anything, is probably pretty close.)  

Note that the recent (or about to happen) X11 changes in the ports stuff 
hasn't been accounted for here...but I think it's right.  Maybe.

Fix: #!/bin/csh -f
# Uuencoded gz-compressed .tar file created by csh script  uufiles
# For more info (11/95), see e.g. http://xxx.lanl.gov/faq/uufaq.html
# If you are on a unix machine this file will unpack itself: strip
# any mail header and call resulting file, e.g., asapm.uu
# (uudecode ignores these header lines and starts at begin line below)
# Then say        csh asapm.uu
# or explicitly execute the commands (generally more secure):
#    uudecode asapm.uu ;   gunzip asapm.tar.gz ;
#    tar -xvf asapm.tar
# On some non-unix (e.g. VAX/VMS), first use editor to change filename
# in "begin" line below to asapm.tar-gz , then execute
#    uudecode asapm.uu
#    gzip -d asapm.tar-gz
#    tar -xvf asapm.tar
#
uudecode $0
chmod 644 asapm.tar.gz
gunzip -c asapm.tar.gz | tar -xvf -
rm $0 asapm.tar.gz
exit
